item.title: A dump for my husband. Today is 50 days since he passed.
item.description: It’s been 50 days since cancer took him from me, and at only 50 years old. These are 50 of the stolen memes I sent to him. He was my hero, my best friend, the love of my life. My favorite. I miss him in every way, but especially in the little, daily shit. #fuckcancer *edit~ thank you all for such kind, loving, and funny comments. And a most viral too…? I love this community. Thank you for celebrating him with me ❤️
item.dc:creator: tlbohon

item.title: America Memes Vol 9
item.description: It's worth noting that some of these might be a bit dated but felt worth posting. As always I'm clearing out some of the memes I spent a frankly absurd amount of time organizing into folders on my phone. As always, I Hope you all enjoyed. I used to post all of the links to my past posts below but the ends of these posts were getting a bit unwieldy. So, at the risk of overcomplicating things I've made a few (sometimes pretty loosely) categorized repositories of my past posts if you'd like to see more I'll link to those below. Movies, TV, and More https://imgur.com/gallery/qWQKZr5 Books, Comics, and More https://imgur.com/gallery/0unBaDX Gaming, Tech, and More https://imgur.com/gallery/17tib1E Food, Family, and Animals https://imgur.com/gallery/zlsjOQa Work, Politics and America https://imgur.com/gallery/zQrWXQE ADHD, Autism, Mental Health, and Millenial https://imgur.com/gallery/G1E6yWd NSFW Memes https://imgur.com/gallery/2I9HXEp Reactions https://imgur.com/gallery/uDjMX0P
item.dc:creator: GeeeButtersnaps

item.title: In the heart of the wilderness, something extraordinary unfolded.
item.description: Source https://hasanjasim.online/bees-swarm-rhino-peaceful-rest-on-giant-beast-natures-sanctuary/ A swarm of bees on a mission to build a new home paused to rest. But instead of perching on a branch, wall, or tree, they chose a living, breathing island: A Rhinoceros. Swarming bees aren’t aggressive. They’re tired, vulnerable, but fiercely protective of their queen if they perceive a threat (so make sure you aren't one). As they search for the perfect spot to start a new colony, they often stop to regroup. Yet it’s almost unheard of to see them settle on the thick, armored back of one of Earth’s largest land mammals. The Rhino is like "yeah what ever".
item.dc:creator: Rastifaan

item.dc:creator: Atrickissomethingawhoredoesformoneyorcocaine

item.title: BOROWITZ: Please give us whatever deal you gave Ghislaine
item.description: KYIV (The Borowitz Report)—Seeking robust security guarantees for his nation, on Wednesday Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy asked Donald J. Trump to “give Ukraine the same protection you have given pedophiles.” Hoping to flatter Trump, Zelenskyy said, “Mr. President, the Ukrainian people ask you to stand strong with them, just as you have stood strong in your refusal to release the Epstein files.” “We are not, of course, a nation of pedophiles,” he continued. “But imagine that we were, and protect us accordingly.” Specifying the sort of protection he sought for Ukraine, Zelenskyy ended his charm offensive by beseeching Trump, “Please give us whatever deal you gave Ghislaine.”
item.dc:creator: JasonM1

item.title: Castellum Divisorium engineers were ahead of their time.
item.description: The Castellum Divisorium was a Roman hydraulic engineering structure—essentially a central water distribution tank or basin. Once water reached the city (via aqueducts), the castellum divisorium would divide the flow into multiple channels, usually leading to public fountains, baths, and private users. Role of Engineers Roman engineers in charge of the castellum divisorium had to: Design hydraulic distribution: ensuring proportional flow to different outlets by using calibrated bronze orifices (calices) that regulated water volume. Maintain pressure balance: controlling the head of water so that distant or elevated districts still received supply. Inspect flow integrity: preventing unauthorized tapping or widening of the outlet pipes, which was a common form of water theft. Oversee construction: building durable cisterns of concrete faced with opus caementicium, often lined with waterproof pozzolana mortar. Document allocations: engineers worked with the curatores aquarum (officials of the aqueducts) to record official water rights and monitor delivery. Notable Figures Frontinus (1st c. CE), author of De Aquaeductu Urbis Romae, describes castella divisorium in detail and the duties of water engineers in Rome. Local municipal engineers (sometimes attached to the collegia fabrum) were tasked with upkeep in provincial towns. Engineering Principles Gravity-fed system: No pumps—flow depended entirely on altitude differentials. Three-tiered division: typically, one channel for public fountains (first priority), one for baths, and one for private concessions. Modular orifices: each bronze outlet had a fixed diameter, corresponding to a legal unit of water (the quinaria), ensuring fairness and traceability.
item.dc:creator: ArcadesCinza

item.title: Ms. Rachel, ever the beacon of humanity, a shining example, and a ray of hope.
item.description: Another hot take: Groups like the ADL and “StopAntisemitism” are crying wolf. By slapping the label of antisemitism on pro-Israel politics that have nothing to do with hatred of Jews, they’ve drained the word of its power. When everything is called antisemitism, nothing is. And that means when real antisemitism appears, people won’t believe it, leaving Jewish communities more vulnerable than ever.
item.dc:creator: cheekysheikhy

item.title: Classical SMACKdown
item.description: Mozart's first name should have been Wolfgangsta. Sauce: https://youtube.com/shorts/H-ooFrcYqPQ Thank you for the smiles Ryze Hendricks! I hope you smile or crack a mental smirk at some point today, from this or an unrelated thing - even if only a brief moment. Cheers!!

item.title: Who knows the struggle of women more than a corporate oil and gas attorney?
item.dc:creator: countervail247365

item.description: In 1969, a secretary accidentally invented the correction fluid that would save millions of students from rewriting entire papers. But Bette Nesmith Graham's journey from single mom to millionaire inventor is one of the most inspiring underdog stories in business history... Bette Nesmith Graham was a single mother working as a secretary in Dallas in the 1950s when electric typewriters made her job much harder. Unlike older typewriters, mistakes on electric models couldn't be easily erased, forcing secretaries to retype entire pages for single errors. Graham watched artists painting a bank's holiday windows and had a brilliant idea. In her kitchen, she mixed white tempera paint with a common solvent to create a correction fluid. She secretly used it at work for months, painting over her typing mistakes and letting them dry before typing the correction on top. When other secretaries noticed her error-free work, they begged for the formula. Graham began mixing batches in her kitchen blender and selling bottles from her home. She called it 'Mistake Out,' later renamed 'Liquid Paper.' Despite being fired from her secretarial job for spending too much time on her side business, she persisted. By 1968, she was producing a million bottles annually from a converted garage. In 1979, Graham sold Liquid Paper to Gillette Corporation for $47.5 million plus royalties. She used her wealth to establish foundations supporting women in business and the arts. Interestingly, her son Mike Nesmith became famous as one of The Monkees, but even he admitted his mother's invention had more lasting impact than his music career.
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007
